RelationshipUpdateIn.description:
Request body for POST /api/v1/relationships/{relationship_id}/update.

`note` is required. `follow_up_after`, `step_slug`, and `advance_step` are
optional side effects. Whether `follow_up_after` was supplied is detected via
model_fields_set so an explicit null (clear the date) is distinct from omitting
the field (leave it unchanged). `step_slug` and `advance_step: true` are
mutually exclusive.
RelationshipUpdateIn.title: RelationshipUpdateIn

ResponsibleItem.description:
One item in /api/v1/responsible.

kind="person" items are Membership-backed and populate person+holon.
kind="holon" items are HolonRelationship-backed and populate from_holon+to_holon.
The two kinds are not forced into a shared subject/object shape because
HolonRelationship is symmetric (no inherent "subject" side) while
Membership is not (a Person belongs to a Holon).
ResponsibleItem.title: ResponsibleItem

info.description:
Public REST API for AI clients and the METIS-hosted MCP adapter.

## Authentication

Two-step flow: POST `/auth/login` with `X-Metis-Api-Key: <API_LOGIN_SECRET>` header + JSON credentials → 24-hour Bearer token. Send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all subsequent requests. Logout: POST `/auth/logout` to revoke immediately.

**Note:** `API_TOKEN` and browser session cookies do NOT authenticate here.

## Error envelope

All application errors return `{code, message, retryable}`. Codes: `unauthenticated` (401), `permission_denied` (403), `not_found` (404), `validation_error` (400, non-retryable), `server_error` (500, retryable). Framework validation failures (422) use django-ninja's default shape instead.

## CORS

No CORS — this surface is designed for server-side AI clients, not browser JS.
info.title: METIS API

post.description:
Requires X-Metis-Api-Key header containing API_LOGIN_SECRET.
On success returns a bearer token valid for 24 hours.
post.summary: Exchange METIS credentials for a 24-hour token
